ref_convention: checked_refs cite SYMBOLS (R/file.R::name, resolved via symbol-lines.json) or TEXT ANCHORS (path::"text") — never bare line numbers. Run dev/scripts/refresh-finding-citations.R --apply to (re)generate resolved_refs; line numbers live only there and are recomputed, so they never go stale in the record.

findings.claim: Only ptr_app, ptr_ui, ptr_ui_controls, and ptr_server accept an unquoted ggplot expression — they each call ptr_capture_formula(rlang::enexpr(formula), envir); ptr_init_state (and thus ptr_extract*/ptr_gg_extra), ptr_id_table, ptr_app_bslib, and ptr_app_grid are string-only.
findings.detail: ptr_init_state does tree <- ptr_translate(formula, expr_check=expr_check) with no enexpr capture; ptr_id_table asserts is.character(formula). So roxygen examples may lead with the expression form ONLY for the four boundary fns; the rest must stay string examples.
findings.source: R/paintr-app.R (ptr_app / ptr_ui / ptr_ui_controls / ptr_server each capture via ptr_capture_formula(rlang::enexpr(formula), envir)); R/paintr-server.R (ptr_init_state: ptr_translate(formula), no capture); R/paintr-input-spec.R (ptr_id_table asserts is.character)
findings.verification: Confirmed the four boundary fns each call ptr_capture_formula(rlang::enexpr(formula), envir) (ptr_app does so twice: formula_str + formula). ptr_init_state body is `tree <- ptr_translate(formula, expr_check=expr_check)` with no capture; ptr_id_table opens with an is.character(formula) assertion. So expression input is exclusive to the four boundary fns.

findings.claim: ptr_capture_formula resolves a bare symbol bound to a string or call by eval_bare in envir then a fallback walk over sys.frames(), so `f <- rlang::expr(ggplot(...)); ptr_ui(f, "id")` works WITHOUT `!!` (and `!!f` also works because enexpr supports injection).
findings.detail: It accepts only character/call/symbol values (guard against poisoning upstream frames), unwraps a top-level { ... } block (keeps last expr), and aborts if a named variable resolves to neither string nor expression. Canonical docs lead with `!!` but bare-symbol resolution is equivalent for a plain variable.
findings.verification: ptr_capture_formula has the is_symbol branch that tryCatch(eval_bare(formula_captured, envir)), then a fallback `for (fr in rev(sys.frames()))`, accepting only character/call/symbol; top-level brace unwrap present. Symbol-to-value resolution path confirmed as claimed.

findings.claim: test-llm.R asserts "every ptr_*() call in the LLM corpus (inst/llm/) names an exported function", so unexporting any function that the corpus documents as a call breaks the suite, and the pre-commit hook (which runs dev/build-llm.R) BLOCKS the commit on that drift.
findings.detail: This is why unexporting ptr_app_bslib/ptr_app_grid rippled far beyond roxygen: the corpus taught them as public L1 entry points across 8 files (overview, primer, level1_ptr_app incl. a whole "Multiple plots" grid section, formula_syntax, level1_ptr_options, level2_ui_text, level3_layout). Pattern: an unexport is an API-surface change that must be propagated to the corpus + generator, not just NAMESPACE.
findings.source: tests/testthat/test-llm.R (corpus-integrity test_that); dev/githooks/pre-commit (runs dev/build-llm.R lint + verify on inst/llm/ or dev/build-llm.R changes)
findings.verification: tests/testthat/test-llm.R::"every ptr_*() call in the corpus names an exported function" (lines 107-118): computes `exports <- getNamespaceExports("ggpaintr")`, scans every llm_corpus_files() file for ptr_*() calls, and `expect_identical(setdiff(calls, c(exports, ID_ALLOWLIST)), character(0))` — so unexporting a corpus-documented function fails the suite. llm_corpus_files() def at line 19. Pre-commit hook dev/githooks/pre-commit lines 86-93: when `inst/llm/` or `dev/build-llm.R` is staged it runs `Rscript dev/build-llm.R lint` (exit-1 on drift) and `verify`, blocking the commit. The session's DEAD_ARGS edit to build-llm.R did not touch this gate. Claim holds.
checked_refs: tests/testthat/test-llm.R::"every ptr_*() call in the corpus names an exported function"

findings.claim: dev/build-llm.R only regenerates two @ptr-gen managed-block types — "token-table" and "topic-index" (both in primer.md) — from hardcoded TOKEN_ROWS/TOPIC_DESC lists; the function-signature bullets in primer.md are plain hand-authored prose (there is NO "level-table" managed block).
findings.detail: Commands: lint (HARD-fails on any non-export ptr_*() call, exit 1), generate (rewrite managed blocks in place), verify (sync gate, exit 1 on drift). Because TOPIC_DESC is hardcoded, removing a function reference requires editing BOTH the .md prose AND the TOPIC_DESC string in build-llm.R, else generate reintroduces the stale ref. lint is the authoritative corpus gate, not just verify.
findings.source: dev/build-llm.R (render_block switch handles only token-table/topic-index; TOKEN_ROWS + TOPIC_DESC hardcoded; lint/generate/verify dispatch)
findings.verification: dev/build-llm.R: render_block switch handles exactly token-table + topic-index (no level-table); TOKEN_ROWS + TOPIC_DESC are hardcoded named vectors; lint/verify are the corpus self-gates. Mechanism intact at HEAD; refs re-stamped as anchors (dev script not in the R-only symbol manifest).

findings.claim: ptr_app_bslib() and ptr_app_grid() were unexported (changed @export to @keywords internal), which keeps their man/*.Rd generated so existing [ptr_app_grid()]/[ptr_app_bslib()] cross-reference links still resolve, while dropping them from NAMESPACE.
findings.detail: Tests call them directly (e.g. ptr_app_grid_components, test-app-bslib.R) and still pass because devtools::test() loads internals. Full suite green at a675550: FAIL 0 / WARN 4 / SKIP 0 / PASS 3523. The two functions remain reachable via ggpaintr:::.
findings.source: NAMESPACE (no export of either); R/paintr-app-bslib.R (ptr_app_bslib, @keywords internal); R/paintr-app.R (ptr_app_grid, @keywords internal); man/ptr_app_bslib.Rd + man/ptr_app_grid.Rd still generated; commits d4d0656, a675550
findings.verification: NAMESPACE has no export of either; @keywords internal present on both; man/ptr_app_bslib.Rd and man/ptr_app_grid.Rd both still generated. Full-suite run on committed a675550 = FAIL 0/WARN 4/SKIP 0/PASS 3523 (721s, serial, browser on).

findings.claim: A bare ggplot(...) call in a non-interactive @examples block runs under R CMD check because package examples execute with ggpaintr (and its attached ggplot2) loaded, so expression-form examples need no library(ggplot2) and no interactive() wrapper to pass run_examples.
findings.detail: Confirmed by the pre-existing unwrapped ptr_ui example (its @examples uses bare ggplot, no interactive()) and by run_examples passing after converting ptr_ui_controls to the expression form. Shiny-app-launching examples still need if (interactive()).
findings.source: R/paintr-app.R (ptr_ui roxygen @examples uses unwrapped bare ggplot); devtools::run_examples()
findings.verification: ptr_ui's @examples block contains an unwrapped `ptr_ui(ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = ppVar, y = ppVar)) + geom_point(), "plot1")` (no interactive(), no library(ggplot2)). devtools::run_examples(run_dontrun=FALSE) printed RUN_EXAMPLES_OK this session after the expression-form conversions.
